message:
style(packs/dns): rustfmt collapses single-line safe-pattern macro

rustfmt rewrites the multi-line `safe_pattern!(...)` macro
invocation back onto one line because both arguments fit comfortably
inside the project's column limit.

The macro call landed in the previous commit as:

    safe_pattern!(
        "dns-dig-safe",
        r"\bdig\b(?!.*(?i:\b(?:axfr|ixfr)\b))"
    ),

…because that's how the previous diff happened to break — the regex
got long enough during the case-insensitive fix that I felt safer
indenting it on its own line. rustfmt disagrees: with the new regex
length, the single-line form is still under the column limit and
matches the formatting of the other two safe patterns in the same
vec. Net change: identical contents, fewer lines.

Pure formatting; no semantic effect.

files.patch:
@@ -26,10 +26,7 @@ pub fn create_pack() -> Pack {
 
 fn create_safe_patterns() -> Vec<SafePattern> {
     vec![
-        safe_pattern!(
-            "dns-dig-safe",
-            r"\bdig\b(?!.*(?i:\b(?:axfr|ixfr)\b))"
-        ),
+        safe_pattern!("dns-dig-safe", r"\bdig\b(?!.*(?i:\b(?:axfr|ixfr)\b))"),
         safe_pattern!("dns-host-safe", r"\bhost\b"),
         safe_pattern!("dns-nslookup-safe", r"\bnslookup\b"),
     ]
